column4: Behold, {{c1::I}} set before you this day a blessing and a curse:
{{c1::Behold}}, I set before you this day a blessing and a curse:
the blessing, if ye shall hearken unto the commandments of the LORD your God, which {{c1::I}} command you this day;
the {{c1::blessing}}, if ye shall hearken unto the commandments of the LORD your God, which I command you this day;
and the curse, if ye shall not hearken unto the commandments of the LORD your God, but turn aside out of the way which {{c1::I}} command you this day, to go after other gods, which ye have not known.
and the curse, if ye shall not {{c1::hearken}} unto the commandments of the LORD your God, but turn aside out of the way which I command you this day, to go after other gods, which ye have not known.

And it shall come to pass, when the LORD thy God shall bring thee into the land whither thou goest to possess it, that thou shalt set the blessing upon mount {{c1::Gerizim}}, and the curse upon mount Ebal.

And it shall come to pass, when the LORD thy God shall bring thee into the land whither thou goest to possess it, that thou shalt set the blessing upon mount Gerizim, and the curse upon mount {{c1::Ebal}}.
Are they not beyond the {{c1::Jordan}}, behind the way of the going down of the sun, in the land of the Canaanites that dwell in the Arabah, over against Gilgal, beside the terebinths of Moreh?
Are they not beyond the Jordan, behind the way of the going down of the sun, in the land of the {{c1::Canaanites}} that dwell in the Arabah, over against Gilgal, beside the terebinths of Moreh?
For ye are to pass over the {{c1::Jordan}} to go in to possess the land which the LORD your God giveth you, and ye shall possess it, and dwell therein.
For ye are to pass over the Jordan to go in to {{c1::possess}} the land which the LORD your God giveth you, and ye shall possess it, and dwell therein.
And ye shall observe to do all the statutes and the ordinances which {{c1::I}} set before you this day.
And ye shall {{c1::observe}} to do all the statutes and the ordinances which I set before you this day.

These are the {{c1::statutes}} and the ordinances, which ye shall observe to do in the land which the LORD, the God of thy fathers, hath given thee to possess it, all the days that ye live upon the earth.

These are the statutes and the {{c1::ordinances}}, which ye shall observe to do in the land which the LORD, the God of thy fathers, hath given thee to possess it, all the days that ye live upon the earth.
Ye shall {{c1::surely}} destroy all the places, wherein the nations that ye are to dispossess served their gods, upon the high mountains, and upon the hills, and under every leafy tree.
Ye shall surely {{c1::destroy}} all the places, wherein the nations that ye are to dispossess served their gods, upon the high mountains, and upon the hills, and under every leafy tree.

And ye shall break down their altars, and dash in pieces their pillars, and burn their {{c1::Asherim}} with fire; and ye shall hew down the graven images of their gods; and ye shall destroy their name out of that place.

And ye shall break down their {{c1::altars}}, and dash in pieces their pillars, and burn their Asherim with fire; and ye shall hew down the graven images of their gods; and ye shall destroy their name out of that place.
But unto the place which the LORD your God shall {{c1::choose}} out of all your tribes to put His name there, even unto His habitation shall ye seek, and thither thou shalt come;
But unto the place which the LORD your God shall choose out of all your {{c1::tribes}} to put His name there, even unto His habitation shall ye seek, and thither thou shalt come;

and {{c1::thither}} ye shall bring your burnt-offerings, and your sacrifices, and your tithes, and the offering of your hand, and your vows, and your freewill-offerings, and the firstlings of your herd and of your flock;

and thither ye shall bring your burnt-{{c1::offerings}}, and your sacrifices, and your tithes, and the offering of your hand, and your vows, and your freewill-offerings, and the firstlings of your herd and of your flock;
and there ye shall eat {{c1::before}} the LORD your God, and ye shall rejoice in all that ye put your hand unto, ye and your households, wherein the LORD thy God hath blessed thee.
and there ye shall eat before the LORD your God, and ye shall {{c1::rejoice}} in all that ye put your hand unto, ye and your households, wherein the LORD thy God hath blessed thee.
Ye shall not do after all that we do here this day, every man {{c1::whatsoever}} is right in his own eyes;
for ye are not as yet come to the rest and to the {{c1::inheritance}}, which the LORD your God giveth thee.
for ye are not as yet come to the rest and to the inheritance, which the LORD your God {{c1::giveth}} thee.
But when ye go over the {{c1::Jordan}}, and dwell in the land which the LORD your God causeth you to inherit, and He giveth you rest from all your enemies round about, so that ye dwell in safety;
But when ye go over the Jordan, and dwell in the land which the LORD your God causeth you to inherit, and {{c1::He}} giveth you rest from all your enemies round about, so that ye dwell in safety;

then it shall come to pass that the place which the LORD your God shall choose to cause His name to dwell there, thither shall ye bring all that {{c1::I}} command you: your burnt-offerings, and your sacrifices, your tithes, and the offering of your hand, and all your choice vows which ye vow unto the LORD.

then it shall come to pass that the place which the LORD your God shall {{c1::choose}} to cause His name to dwell there, thither shall ye bring all that I command you: your burnt-offerings, and your sacrifices, your tithes, and the offering of your hand, and all your choice vows which ye vow unto the LORD.

And ye shall rejoice before the LORD your God, ye, and your sons, and your daughters, and your men-servants, and your maid-servants, and the {{c1::Levite}} that is within your gates, forasmuch as he hath no portion nor inheritance with you.

And ye shall {{c1::rejoice}} before the LORD your God, ye, and your sons, and your daughters, and your men-servants, and your maid-servants, and the Levite that is within your gates, forasmuch as he hath no portion nor inheritance with you.
Take heed to {{c1::thyself}} that thou offer not thy burnt-offerings in every place that thou seest;
Take heed to thyself that thou offer not thy burnt-{{c1::offerings}} in every place that thou seest;
but in the place which the LORD shall choose in one of thy tribes, there thou shalt offer thy burnt-offerings, and there thou shalt do all that {{c1::I}} command thee.
but in the place which the LORD shall {{c1::choose}} in one of thy tribes, there thou shalt offer thy burnt-offerings, and there thou shalt do all that I command thee.

Notwithstanding thou mayest kill and eat flesh within all thy gates, after all the desire of thy soul, according to the blessing of the LORD thy God which {{c1::He}} hath given thee; the unclean and the clean may eat thereof, as of the gazelle, and as of the hart.

{{c1::Notwithstanding}} thou mayest kill and eat flesh within all thy gates, after all the desire of thy soul, according to the blessing of the LORD thy God which He hath given thee; the unclean and the clean may eat thereof, as of the gazelle, and as of the hart.

Thou {{c1::mayest}} not eat within thy gates the tithe of thy corn, or of thy wine, or of thine oil, or the firstlings of thy herd or of thy flock, nor any of thy vows which thou vowest, nor thy freewill-offerings, nor the offering of thy hand;

Thou mayest not eat {{c1::within}} thy gates the tithe of thy corn, or of thy wine, or of thine oil, or the firstlings of thy herd or of thy flock, nor any of thy vows which thou vowest, nor thy freewill-offerings, nor the offering of thy hand;

but thou shalt eat them before the LORD thy God in the place which the LORD thy God shall choose, thou, and thy son, and thy daughter, and thy man-servant, and thy maid-servant, and the {{c1::Levite}} that is within thy gates; and thou shalt rejoice before the LORD thy God in all that thou puttest thy hand unto.

but thou shalt eat them {{c1::before}} the LORD thy God in the place which the LORD thy God shall choose, thou, and thy son, and thy daughter, and thy man-servant, and thy maid-servant, and the Levite that is within thy gates; and thou shalt rejoice before the LORD thy God in all that thou puttest thy hand unto.
Take heed to thyself that thou forsake not the {{c1::Levite}} as long as thou livest upon thy land.
Take heed to {{c1::thyself}} that thou forsake not the Levite as long as thou livest upon thy land.

When the LORD thy God shall enlarge thy border, as {{c1::He}} hath promised thee, and thou shalt say: ‘I will eat flesh’, because thy soul desireth to eat flesh; thou mayest eat flesh, after all the desire of thy soul.

When the LORD thy God shall enlarge thy border, as He hath promised thee, and thou shalt say: ‘{{c1::I}} will eat flesh’, because thy soul desireth to eat flesh; thou mayest eat flesh, after all the desire of thy soul.

If the place which the LORD thy God shall choose to put His name there be too far from thee, then thou shalt kill of thy herd and of thy flock, which the LORD hath given thee, as {{c1::I}} have commanded thee, and thou shalt eat within thy gates, after all the desire of thy soul.

If the place which the LORD thy God shall {{c1::choose}} to put His name there be too far from thee, then thou shalt kill of thy herd and of thy flock, which the LORD hath given thee, as I have commanded thee, and thou shalt eat within thy gates, after all the desire of thy soul.
{{c1::Howbeit}} as the gazelle and as the hart is eaten, so thou shalt eat thereof; the unclean and the clean may eat thereof alike.
Howbeit as the {{c1::gazelle}} and as the hart is eaten, so thou shalt eat thereof; the unclean and the clean may eat thereof alike.
Only be {{c1::stedfast}} in not eating the blood; for the blood is the life; and thou shalt not eat the life with the flesh.
Only be stedfast in not {{c1::eating}} the blood; for the blood is the life; and thou shalt not eat the life with the flesh.
Thou shalt not eat it; that it may go well with thee, and with thy {{c1::children}} after thee, when thou shalt do that which is right in the eyes of the LORD.
Only thy holy {{c1::things}} which thou hast, and thy vows, thou shalt take, and go unto the place which the LORD shall choose;
Only thy holy things which thou hast, and thy vows, thou shalt take, and go unto the place which the LORD shall {{c1::choose}};

and thou shalt offer thy burnt-{{c1::offerings}}, the flesh and the blood, upon the altar of the LORD thy God; and the blood of thy sacrifices shall be poured out against the altar of the LORD thy God, and thou shalt eat the flesh.

and thou shalt offer thy burnt-offerings, the flesh and the blood, upon the altar of the LORD thy God; and the blood of thy {{c1::sacrifices}} shall be poured out against the altar of the LORD thy God, and thou shalt eat the flesh.

Observe and hear all these words which {{c1::I}} command thee, that it may go well with thee, and with thy children after thee for ever, when thou doest that which is good and right in the eyes of the LORD thy God.

{{c1::Observe}} and hear all these words which I command thee, that it may go well with thee, and with thy children after thee for ever, when thou doest that which is good and right in the eyes of the LORD thy God.
When the LORD thy God shall cut off the {{c1::nations}} from before thee, whither thou goest in to dispossess them, and thou dispossessest them, and dwellest in their land;
When the LORD thy God shall cut off the nations from {{c1::before}} thee, whither thou goest in to dispossess them, and thou dispossessest them, and dwellest in their land;

take heed to thyself that thou be not ensnared to follow them, after that they are destroyed from before thee; and that thou inquire not after their gods, saying: ‘{{c1::How}} used these nations to serve their gods? even so will I do likewise.’

take heed to thyself that thou be not ensnared to follow them, after that they are destroyed from before thee; and that thou inquire not after their gods, saying: ‘How used these nations to serve their gods? even so will {{c1::I}} do likewise.’

Thou shalt not do so unto the LORD thy God; for every abomination to the LORD, which {{c1::He}} hateth, have they done unto their gods; for even their sons and their daughters do they burn in the fire to their gods.

Thou shalt not do so unto the LORD thy God; for every {{c1::abomination}} to the LORD, which He hateth, have they done unto their gods; for even their sons and their daughters do they burn in the fire to their gods.
All this word which {{c1::I}} command you, that shall ye observe to do; thou shalt not add thereto, nor diminish from it.
All this word which I {{c1::command}} you, that shall ye observe to do; thou shalt not add thereto, nor diminish from it.
If there arise in the midst of thee a {{c1::prophet}}, or a dreamer of dreams—and he give thee a sign or a wonder,
If there arise in the midst of thee a prophet, or a {{c1::dreamer}} of dreams—and he give thee a sign or a wonder,
and the sign or the wonder come to pass, whereof he spoke unto thee—saying: ‘{{c1::Let}} us go after other gods, which thou hast not known, and let us serve them’;
and the sign or the {{c1::wonder}} come to pass, whereof he spoke unto thee—saying: ‘Let us go after other gods, which thou hast not known, and let us serve them’;

thou shalt not {{c1::hearken}} unto the words of that prophet, or unto that dreamer of dreams; for the LORD your God putteth you to proof, to know whether ye do love the LORD your God with all your heart and with all your soul.

thou shalt not hearken unto the words of that {{c1::prophet}}, or unto that dreamer of dreams; for the LORD your God putteth you to proof, to know whether ye do love the LORD your God with all your heart and with all your soul.
After the LORD your God shall ye walk, and Him shall ye fear, and His {{c1::commandments}} shall ye keep, and unto His voice shall ye hearken, and Him shall ye serve, and unto Him shall ye cleave.
After the LORD your God shall ye walk, and Him shall ye fear, and His commandments shall ye keep, and unto His voice shall ye {{c1::hearken}}, and Him shall ye serve, and unto Him shall ye cleave.

And that prophet, or that dreamer of dreams, shall be put to death; because he hath spoken perversion against the LORD your God, who brought you out of the land of {{c1::Egypt}}, and redeemed thee out of the house of bondage, to draw thee aside out of the way which the LORD thy God commanded thee to walk in. So shalt thou put away the evil from the midst of thee.

And that prophet, or that dreamer of dreams, shall be put to death; because he hath spoken perversion against the LORD your God, who brought you out of the land of Egypt, and redeemed thee out of the house of bondage, to draw thee aside out of the way which the LORD thy God commanded thee to walk in. {{c1::So}} shalt thou put away the evil from the midst of thee.

If thy {{c1::brother}}, the son of thy mother, or thy son, or thy daughter, or the wife of thy bosom, or thy friend, that is as thine own soul, entice thee secretly, saying: 'Let us go and serve other gods,' which thou hast not known, thou, nor thy fathers;

If thy brother, the son of thy {{c1::mother}}, or thy son, or thy daughter, or the wife of thy bosom, or thy friend, that is as thine own soul, entice thee secretly, saying: 'Let us go and serve other gods,' which thou hast not known, thou, nor thy fathers;
of the gods of the {{c1::peoples}} that are round about you, nigh unto thee, or far off from thee, from the one end of the earth even unto the other end of the earth;
thou shalt not {{c1::consent}} unto him, nor hearken unto him; neither shall thine eye pity him, neither shalt thou spare, neither shalt thou conceal him;
thou shalt not consent unto him, nor {{c1::hearken}} unto him; neither shall thine eye pity him, neither shalt thou spare, neither shalt thou conceal him;
but thou shalt {{c1::surely}} kill him; thy hand shall be first upon him to put him to death, and afterwards the hand of all the people.
but thou shalt surely kill him; thy hand shall be first upon him to put him to death, and {{c1::afterwards}} the hand of all the people.
And thou shalt stone him with stones, that he die; because he hath sought to draw thee away from the LORD thy God, who brought thee out of the land of {{c1::Egypt}}, out of the house of bondage.
And thou shalt stone him with {{c1::stones}}, that he die; because he hath sought to draw thee away from the LORD thy God, who brought thee out of the land of Egypt, out of the house of bondage.
And all {{c1::Israel}} shall hear, and fear, and shall do no more any such wickedness as this is in the midst of thee.
And all Israel shall hear, and fear, and shall do no more any such {{c1::wickedness}} as this is in the midst of thee.
If thou shalt hear tell {{c1::concerning}} one of thy cities, which the LORD thy God giveth thee to dwell there, saying:
If thou shalt hear tell concerning one of thy {{c1::cities}}, which the LORD thy God giveth thee to dwell there, saying:
’Certain base fellows are gone out from the midst of thee, and have drawn away the inhabitants of their city, saying: {{c1::Let}} us go and serve other gods, which ye have not known’;
’Certain base fellows are gone out from the midst of thee, and have drawn away the inhabitants of their city, saying: Let us go and serve other gods, which ye have not known’;
then shalt thou {{c1::inquire}}, and make search, and ask diligently; and, behold, if it be truth, and the thing certain, that such abomination is wrought in the midst of thee;
then shalt thou inquire, and make {{c1::search}}, and ask diligently; and, behold, if it be truth, and the thing certain, that such abomination is wrought in the midst of thee;
thou shalt {{c1::surely}} smite the inhabitants of that city with the edge of the sword, destroying it utterly, and all that is therein and the cattle thereof, with the edge of the sword.
thou shalt surely smite the {{c1::inhabitants}} of that city with the edge of the sword, destroying it utterly, and all that is therein and the cattle thereof, with the edge of the sword.

And thou shalt {{c1::gather}} all the spoil of it into the midst of the broad place thereof, and shall burn with fire the city, and all the spoil thereof every whit, unto the LORD thy God; and it shall be a heap for ever; it shall not be built again.

And thou shalt gather all the spoil of it into the midst of the broad place {{c1::thereof}}, and shall burn with fire the city, and all the spoil thereof every whit, unto the LORD thy God; and it shall be a heap for ever; it shall not be built again.

And there shall cleave nought of the devoted thing to thy hand, that the LORD may turn from the fierceness of His anger, and show thee mercy, and have compassion upon thee, and multiply thee, as {{c1::He}} hath sworn unto thy fathers;

And there shall {{c1::cleave}} nought of the devoted thing to thy hand, that the LORD may turn from the fierceness of His anger, and show thee mercy, and have compassion upon thee, and multiply thee, as He hath sworn unto thy fathers;
when thou shalt hearken to the voice of the LORD thy God, to keep all His commandments which {{c1::I}} command thee this day, to do that which is right in the eyes of the LORD thy God.
when thou shalt {{c1::hearken}} to the voice of the LORD thy God, to keep all His commandments which I command thee this day, to do that which is right in the eyes of the LORD thy God.
Ye are the {{c1::children}} of the LORD your God: ye shall not cut yourselves, nor make any baldness between your eyes for the dead.
Ye are the children of the LORD your God: ye shall not cut {{c1::yourselves}}, nor make any baldness between your eyes for the dead.
For thou art a holy {{c1::people}} unto the LORD thy God, and the LORD hath chosen thee to be His own treasure out of all peoples that are upon the face of the earth.
For thou art a holy people unto the LORD thy God, and the LORD hath {{c1::chosen}} thee to be His own treasure out of all peoples that are upon the face of the earth.
Thou shalt not eat any {{c1::abominable}} thing.
These are the {{c1::beasts}} which ye may eat: the ox, the sheep, and the goat,
the hart, and the {{c1::gazelle}}, and the roebuck, and the wild goat, and the pygarg, and the antelope, and the mountain-sheep.
the hart, and the gazelle, and the {{c1::roebuck}}, and the wild goat, and the pygarg, and the antelope, and the mountain-sheep.
And every beast that {{c1::parteth}} the hoof, and hath the hoof wholly cloven in two, and cheweth the cud, among the beasts, that ye may eat.
And every beast that parteth the hoof, and hath the hoof {{c1::wholly}} cloven in two, and cheweth the cud, among the beasts, that ye may eat.

{{c1::Nevertheless}} these ye shall not eat of them that only chew the cud, or of them that only have the hoof cloven: the camel, and the hare, and the rock-badger, because they chew the cud but part not the hoof, they are unclean unto you;

Nevertheless these ye shall not eat of them that only chew the cud, or of them that only have the hoof {{c1::cloven}}: the camel, and the hare, and the rock-badger, because they chew the cud but part not the hoof, they are unclean unto you;
and the swine, {{c1::because}} he parteth the hoof but cheweth not the cud, he is unclean unto you; of their flesh ye shall not eat, and their carcasses ye shall not touch.
and the swine, because he {{c1::parteth}} the hoof but cheweth not the cud, he is unclean unto you; of their flesh ye shall not eat, and their carcasses ye shall not touch.
These ye may eat of all that are in the {{c1::waters}}: whatsoever hath fins and scales may ye eat;
These ye may eat of all that are in the waters: {{c1::whatsoever}} hath fins and scales may ye eat;
and {{c1::whatsoever}} hath not fins and scales ye shall not eat; it is unclean unto you.
and whatsoever hath not fins and {{c1::scales}} ye shall not eat; it is unclean unto you.
But these are they of which ye shall not eat: the great {{c1::vulture}}, and the bearded vulture, and the ospray;
But these are they of which ye shall not eat: the great vulture, and the {{c1::bearded}} vulture, and the ospray;
and the glede, and the {{c1::falcon}}, and the kite after its kinds;
and the {{c1::ostrich}}, and the night-hawk, and the sea-mew, and the hawk after its kinds;
the {{c1::little}} owl, and the great owl, and the horned owl;
the little owl, and the great owl, and the {{c1::horned}} owl;
and the {{c1::pelican}}, and the carrion-vulture, and the cormorant;
and the pelican, and the {{c1::carrion}}-vulture, and the cormorant;
and the stork, and the heron after its kinds, and the {{c1::hoopoe}}, and the bat.
And all {{c1::winged}} swarming things are unclean unto you; they shall not be eaten.
And all winged {{c1::swarming}} things are unclean unto you; they shall not be eaten.
Of all clean {{c1::winged}} things ye may eat.
Of all clean winged {{c1::things}} ye may eat.

Ye shall not eat of any thing that dieth of itself; thou mayest give it unto the stranger that is within thy gates, that he may eat it; or thou mayest sell it unto a foreigner; for thou art a holy people unto the LORD thy God. {{c1::Thou}} shalt not seethe a kid in its mother’s milk.

Ye shall not eat of any thing that dieth of {{c1::itself}}; thou mayest give it unto the stranger that is within thy gates, that he may eat it; or thou mayest sell it unto a foreigner; for thou art a holy people unto the LORD thy God. Thou shalt not seethe a kid in its mother’s milk.
Thou shalt {{c1::surely}} tithe all the increase of thy seed, that which is brought forth in the field year by year.
Thou shalt surely tithe all the {{c1::increase}} of thy seed, that which is brought forth in the field year by year.

And thou shalt eat before the LORD thy God, in the place which {{c1::He}} shall choose to cause His name to dwell there, the tithe of thy corn, of thy wine, and of thine oil, and the firstlings of thy herd and of thy flock; that thou mayest learn to fear the LORD thy God always.

And thou shalt eat {{c1::before}} the LORD thy God, in the place which He shall choose to cause His name to dwell there, the tithe of thy corn, of thy wine, and of thine oil, and the firstlings of thy herd and of thy flock; that thou mayest learn to fear the LORD thy God always.

And if the way be too long for thee, so that thou art not able to carry it, {{c1::because}} the place is too far from thee, which the LORD thy God shall choose to set His name there, when the LORD thy God shall bless thee;

And if the way be too long for thee, so that thou art not able to carry it, because the place is too far from thee, which the LORD thy God shall {{c1::choose}} to set His name there, when the LORD thy God shall bless thee;
then shalt thou turn it into money, and bind up the money in thy hand, and shalt go unto the place which the LORD thy God shall {{c1::choose}}.

And thou shalt {{c1::bestow}} the money for whatsoever thy soul desireth, for oxen, or for sheep, or for wine, or for strong drink, or for whatsoever thy soul asketh of thee; and thou shalt eat there before the LORD thy God, and thou shalt rejoice, thou and thy household.

And thou shalt bestow the money for {{c1::whatsoever}} thy soul desireth, for oxen, or for sheep, or for wine, or for strong drink, or for whatsoever thy soul asketh of thee; and thou shalt eat there before the LORD thy God, and thou shalt rejoice, thou and thy household.
And the {{c1::Levite}} that is within thy gates, thou shalt not forsake him; for he hath no portion nor inheritance with thee.
And the Levite that is {{c1::within}} thy gates, thou shalt not forsake him; for he hath no portion nor inheritance with thee.
At the end of every three years, even in the same year, thou shalt bring forth all the tithe of thine {{c1::increase}}, and shall lay it up within thy gates.
At the end of every three years, even in the same year, thou shalt bring forth all the tithe of thine increase, and shall lay it up {{c1::within}} thy gates.

And the {{c1::Levite}}, because he hath no portion nor inheritance with thee, and the stranger, and the fatherless, and the widow, that are within thy gates, shall come, and shall eat and be satisfied; that the LORD thy God may bless thee in all the work of thy hand which thou doest.

And the Levite, {{c1::because}} he hath no portion nor inheritance with thee, and the stranger, and the fatherless, and the widow, that are within thy gates, shall come, and shall eat and be satisfied; that the LORD thy God may bless thee in all the work of thy hand which thou doest.
At the end of every seven years thou shalt make a {{c1::release}}.

And this is the manner of the release: every creditor shall release that which he hath lent unto his neighbour; he shall not exact it of his neighbour and his brother; because the {{c1::LORD’S}} release hath been proclaimed.

And this is the {{c1::manner}} of the release: every creditor shall release that which he hath lent unto his neighbour; he shall not exact it of his neighbour and his brother; because the LORD’S release hath been proclaimed.
Of a {{c1::foreigner}} thou mayest exact it; but whatsoever of thine is with thy brother thy hand shall release.
Of a foreigner thou {{c1::mayest}} exact it; but whatsoever of thine is with thy brother thy hand shall release.
{{c1::Howbeit}} there shall be no needy among you—for the LORD will surely bless thee in the land which the LORD thy God giveth thee for an inheritance to possess it—
Howbeit there shall be no needy among you—for the LORD will {{c1::surely}} bless thee in the land which the LORD thy God giveth thee for an inheritance to possess it—
if only thou diligently hearken unto the voice of the LORD thy God, to observe to do all this commandment which {{c1::I}} command thee this day.
if only thou {{c1::diligently}} hearken unto the voice of the LORD thy God, to observe to do all this commandment which I command thee this day.

For the LORD thy God will bless thee, as {{c1::He}} promised thee; and thou shalt lend unto many nations, but thou shalt not borrow; and thou shalt rule over many nations, but they shall not rule over thee.

For the LORD thy God will bless thee, as He {{c1::promised}} thee; and thou shalt lend unto many nations, but thou shalt not borrow; and thou shalt rule over many nations, but they shall not rule over thee.

If there be among you a needy man, one of thy {{c1::brethren}}, within any of thy gates, in thy land which the LORD thy God giveth thee, thou shalt not harden thy heart, nor shut thy hand from thy needy brother;

If there be among you a needy man, one of thy brethren, {{c1::within}} any of thy gates, in thy land which the LORD thy God giveth thee, thou shalt not harden thy heart, nor shut thy hand from thy needy brother;
but thou shalt {{c1::surely}} open thy hand unto him, and shalt surely lend him sufficient for his need in that which he wanteth.
but thou shalt surely open thy hand unto him, and shalt surely lend him {{c1::sufficient}} for his need in that which he wanteth.

{{c1::Beware}} that there be not a base thought in thy heart, saying: ‘The seventh year, the year of release, is at hand’; and thine eye be evil against thy needy brother, and thou give him nought; and he cry unto the LORD against thee, and it be sin in thee.

Beware that there be not a base {{c1::thought}} in thy heart, saying: ‘The seventh year, the year of release, is at hand’; and thine eye be evil against thy needy brother, and thou give him nought; and he cry unto the LORD against thee, and it be sin in thee.

Thou shalt {{c1::surely}} give him, and thy heart shall not be grieved when thou givest unto him; because that for this thing the LORD thy God will bless thee in all thy work, and in all that thou puttest thy hand unto.

If thy brother, a {{c1::Hebrew}} man, or a Hebrew woman, be sold unto thee, he shall serve thee six years; and in the seventh year thou shalt let him go free from thee.

All the {{c1::firstling}} males that are born of thy herd and of thy flock thou shalt sanctify unto the LORD thy God; thou shalt do no work with the firstling of thine ox, nor shear the firstling of thy flock.
